title: parahellenia malipoensis
intro: Parahellenia malipoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the Malipo parahellenia. It is native to Yunnan Province in China, where it grows in the wild at high elevations. The medicinal properties of Parahellenia malipoensis include its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its herbal preparations may also be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
